channel.title: The Trading Journal
channel.description: A daily log of trades, lessons, and probabilities

item.title: Calendar Call Spread: Same Strike, Different Expiries, Theta in the Middle
item.description: Anatomy of the calendar call spread: a two-leg same-strike time spread that profits when the underlying parks near the strike at the front-month's expiry. The pure theta-harvest structure in the playbook, defined-risk by construction.

item.title: DRAM Calendar Call Spread — Dec 18 / Jan 15 '27 70: Theta Harvest on a Selloff Recovery
item.description: Opened a DRAM calendar call spread at $70 strike, short Dec 18 / long Jan 15 '27, on continued DRAM weakness. Net debit $107.50/contract, defined-risk theta-harvest structure with $628 estimated max profit if DRAM reclaims $70 by Dec 18 expiry.

item.title: Vol Expansion Setup: Long Strangle on SPX Ahead of FOMC
item.description: SPX in a transition regime between its 50-day and 200-day averages ahead of FOMC. Long strangle Sep 4, strikes $6,900/$7,950, debit ~$4,950/contract.

item.title: Long Call Condor Strike Selection: How to Pick the Body Width
item.description: The decision tree for choosing a long call condor's body width: 85 points vs 110 vs 130 vs 200. Why three different bodies were used on the same underlying in one trading session. A rule-based selection framework that maps regime and thesis to strike geometry.

item.title: Long Call Condor vs Iron Condor: When to Use Each
item.description: The two range-bound structures in the playbook — iron condor and long call condor — side by side. Capital efficiency vs theta collection. When to pick the all-call structure, when to pick the put-inclusive structure, and why three long call condors entered July 23 with no iron condors.

item.title: Long Call Condor: The All-Call Range-Bound, Theta-Positive Vol-Skew Trade
item.description: Anatomy of the long call condor — four call legs that harvest theta and call-side vol skew when the underlying stays inside a defined range. The all-call counterpart to the iron condor: same payoff shape, different Greek profile, narrower body tolerance.

item.title: Tredey Market Gauge: Three-Index Composite Snapshot
item.description: A daily overbought/oversold snapshot for the S&P 500, NASDAQ-100, and Russell 2000. Each index gets a 0-100 reading from the Tredey Composite Gauge — six daily indicators equal-weighted into a single number.

item.title: Common Options Math Errors I Make (and How I Catch Them)
item.description: Two recurring math errors in options-spread writing: the long call condor max-profit formula and the OptionStrat basis vs live yfinance discrepancy. Both shipped live before being caught. The derivations, the live examples, and the verification routines that catch them next time.

item.title: QQQ Call Skew: How to Read It and Trade It
item.description: Why QQQ has more call skew than SPX, how to measure call skew from a live option chain, when skew widens (and when it compresses), and how the long call condor trades it. Includes the live IV surface from three July 23 trades as case studies.

item.title: QQQ Long Call Condor — Nov 20 '26 715/725/810/820: Narrow-Body Range-Bound, 120 DTE
item.description: Opened a QQQ long call condor (Nov 20 '26, 715/725/810/820), pairing a near-ATM bull call credit spread (715/725) with a deep-OTM bear call credit spread (810/820) to express rangebound sideways drift over Q3 earnings season. $355 defined risk; max profit $645 in $725–$810 zone.

item.title: QQQ Long Call Condor — Dec 18 '26 700/715/845/860: Wide-Body Range-Bound, Narrow-Wing Limited
item.description: Opened a QQQ long call condor (Dec 18 '26, 700/715/845/860), pairing a near-ATM bull call credit spread (700/715) with a deep-OTM bear call credit spread (845/860) to express range-bounded sideways drift. $689.50 defined risk; max profit $810.50 in $715–$845 zone.

item.title: XSP Long Call Condor — Dec 18 '26 690/700/810/820: 5-Month Range-Bound Lower-Vol Structure
item.description: Opened an XSP long call condor (Dec 18 '26, 690/700/810/820), short the body's IV skew while harvesting theta into year-end. $609 defined risk; max profit $391 between $700–$810 (limited by 10-pt wings, not 110-pt body).

item.title: SPX Bull Put Spread — Thursday, July 23, 2026
item.description: SPX closed Wed at $7,498.96. Bull regime intact. SPX Aug 21 AM-settled 7200/7180 20-wide bull put, $360 credit, $1,640 max loss, ~80% POP, FOMC 5 days out.

item.title: NASA Bull Call Spread — Jan 21 '28 30/15: Long-Dated Space-ETF Upside
item.description: Opened a NASA ETF Jan 21 2028 30/15 bull call spread, an 18-month LEAPS upside structure on the Tema Space Innovators ETF. Net debit $6.10, max loss $610/contract × 1 = $610 total. Cushion to long strike 36%, breakeven $21.10.

item.title: RSP Long Call Condor — Jan 21 '28 230/220/290/280: Equal-Weight SPY Trade on a Flat Tape
item.description: Opened an RSP ETF Jan 21 2028 230/220/290/280 long call condor, an 18-month structure expressing the view that the equal-weight S&P 500 will consolidate in a $63-wide profit zone without a major breakout. Net debit $3.175/share, $317.50/contract × 1 = $317.50 total, 1:2.15 risk:reward.

item.title: RSP Short Upper Condor — Jan 21 '28 220/230/280/285: Same Debit, Free Upside Above $287
item.description: Opened a second RSP ETF Jan 21 2028 220/230/280/285 long call condor with a $5-wide upper body (vs $10-wide on RSP #1). Same $3.175/share net debit but a permanent $182.50/contract upside plateau above $287 instead of a $317.50 max loss above $290. Direct comparison to RSP #1 (mBisziPFxRvo) shipped earlier today.

item.title: SPX Bull Put Spread — Wednesday, July 22, 2026
item.description: SPX closed Tuesday at $7,509.20. Bull regime intact (20d +0.5%, breadth 75%, IV-rank 42.5). SPX Aug 21 AM-settled 7235/7225 bull put spread, $240 credit, $760 max loss, ~80% POP, FOMC one week out.

item.title: Tuesday Pre-Market Brief — SPX Long Strangle at $7,443 Spot: $7,095 Put / $7,890 Call, Aug 21 AM-Settled Expiry
item.description: SPX closed Monday at $7,443.28. TRANSITION regime — price between MA50 and MA200, no clear trend. SPX Aug 21 AM-settled long strangle 7095/7890, $4,285 debit, ~32% POP, vol expansion play.

item.title: NASA Bull Put Spread — Jan 15 '27 25/20: Long-Dated Space-ETF Premium
item.description: Opened a NASA ETF Jan 15 2027 25/20 bull put spread, a 178-DTE long-dated short-premium structure on the Tema Space Innovators ETF. Live mid credit $2.525, max loss $247.50/contract × 4 = $990 total. ~60% short-leg POP, ~1¢/day theta.

item.title: Monday Pre-Market Brief — SPX Bull Put Spread at $7,457 Spot: $7,170 / $7,155, Aug 21 AM-Settled Expiry
item.description: SPX closed Friday at $7,457.69. BULL regime with 20d trend still positive but a mild 1.5% 5d pullback. SPX Aug 21 AM-settled 7170/7155 bull put spread, ~$300 credit, ~$1,200 max loss, ~80% POP.
